- 1. Access to public transport by distance and public transport carrying capacity, geography, gender, selected demographic and socio-economic characteristics, and count of public transport stops Table: 23-10-0314-01 Geography: Canada, Province or territory, Census subdivision, Census metropolitan area, Census agglomeration, Census metropolitan area part, Census agglomeration part Frequency: Annual Description: Estimates to support Sustainable Development Goals - Indicator 11.2.1: "Proportion of population that has convenient access to public transport, by sex, age and persons with ... Description: Estimates to support Sustainable Development Goals - Indicator 11.2.1: "Proportion of population that has convenient access to public transport, by sex, age and persons with disabilities" and the Canadian Indicator Framework - Indicator 11.4.1: "Percentage of the population living within 500 meters of a public transport stop." - 4. Residential properties and private dwellings by period of construction, experimental estimates Table: 46-10-0101-01 Geography: Province or territory, Census subdivision, Census metropolitan area, Census agglomeration, Census metropolitan area part, Census agglomeration part Frequency: Annual Description: Number of residential properties and private dwellings, and their assessment value, by period of construction and categories of private dwelling counts. The results are based on an ... Description: Number of residential properties and private dwellings, and their assessment value, by period of construction and categories of private dwelling counts. The results are based on an experimental approach, meaning they are derived using recently developed methodologies and may be subject to revisions. More Release date: 2026-03-31
- 5. Municipal operating costs: Water, waste, recreation and culture Table: 10-10-0173-01 Geography: Census division, Census subdivision, Census agglomeration Frequency: Annual Description: This experimental table presents the annual operating expenses of 216 local governments, classified according to the Canadian Classification of the Functions of Government (CCOFOG) and ... Description: This experimental table presents the annual operating expenses of 216 local governments, classified according to the Canadian Classification of the Functions of Government (CCOFOG) and the Canadian Government Finance Statistics (CGFS) framework. It presents municipal expenses related to rainwater management, water supply, wastewater treatment, waste disposal, culture, leisure and religion for 2020/2021 to 2023/2024. More Release date: 2026-03-31
- 6. Release date: 2026-03-31
- 7. - 9. Rural Canada non-profits, estimates of active non-profit organization counts, revenues and employment by geography Table: 45-10-0107-01 Geography: Canada, Province or territory, Census subdivision Frequency: Annual Description: Estimates of active non-profit organization (NPO) counts, revenues and employment by geographic classification, as well as by rural and small town or functional urban areas. An ... Description: Estimates of active non-profit organization (NPO) counts, revenues and employment by geographic classification, as well as by rural and small town or functional urban areas. An ‘active’ NPO is any NPO that was in operation for the full or partial duration of the reference year and reported revenues and/or employment. More Release date: 2026-03-13
- 10. Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation, housing starts, under construction and completions in large urban areas, annual Table: 34-10-0125-01 Geography: Census subdivision, Census metropolitan area, Census agglomeration Frequency: Annual Description: This table contains data described by the following dimensions (Not all combinations are available): Geography (53 items: Large urban areas; Barrie; Ontario; Belleville; Ontario; ... Description: This table contains data described by the following dimensions (Not all combinations are available): Geography (53 items: Large urban areas; Barrie; Ontario; Belleville; Ontario; Brantford; Ontario ...), Housing estimates (3 items: Housing starts; Housing under construction; Housing completions ...), Type of unit (5 items: Total units; Semi-detached units; Row units; Single-detached units ...). More Release date: 2026-01-21

The Sort by feature provides four options:
- By date – sorts results by product release date starting with the most recent.
- By most popular – gives priority to results that are most frequently selected by users, followed by the most recent.
- By relevance is only needed or beneficial when you use the search box and submit a search query. Results are ordered by a combination of where the search term appears in the content, e.g., if it appears in the title, it likely means the product is more relevant to your search; how up to date or new the content in the product is; how popular the product is; and other factors.
- By title – sorts results alphabetically.
The Sort by feature provides three options:
- By date – sorts results by product release date starting with the most recent.
- By relevance is only needed or beneficial when you use the search box and submit a search query. Results are ordered by a combination of where the search term appears in the content, e.g., if it appears in the title, it likely means the product is more relevant to your search; how up to date or new the content in the product is; how popular the product is; and other factors.
- By title – sorts results alphabetically.
- You can initiate a search by entering keywords or selecting filters (e.g., under Subject, Geography , etc.) on the left side of the page.
You can initiate a search by entering keywords or selecting filters (e.g., under Subject, Geography , etc.) on the left side of the page.
- Filters can be used together or in any combination. Each time you select a filter, the results on the page are updated.
Filters can be used together or in any combination. Each time you select a filter, the results on the page are updated.
- To start a new search, click on the Clear all button above the search box or deselect each filter.
To start a new search, click on the Clear all button above the search box or deselect each filter.
- Specified keywords and filters appear above the search box. You can deselect any or all the tags to refine or clear your search.
Specified keywords and filters appear above the search box. You can deselect any or all the tags to refine or clear your search.
- You can enter keywords in the search box. It is not necessary to use “+” or “, “ or “AND”.
You can enter keywords in the search box. It is not necessary to use “+” or “, “ or “AND”.
- You can delete some or all of the keywords from your search string.
You can delete some or all of the keywords from your search string.
- Quotes around keywords limit the search to a specific phrase. For example, “labour force survey” will limit results to products containing this phrase.
Quotes around keywords limit the search to a specific phrase.
- For example, “labour force survey” will limit results to products containing this phrase.
For example, “labour force survey” will limit results to products containing this phrase.
- Use “or” between keywords to obtain results that contain one or more search terms. For example, labour or­ force or survey will limit results to products containing any or all of these words.
Use “or” between keywords to obtain results that contain one or more search terms.
- For example, labour or­ force or survey will limit results to products containing any or all of these words.
For example, labour or­ force or survey will limit results to products containing any or all of these words.
- This search looks for results that have your query term in the title, description, subject, geography, product number or other information about the product. For example, when searching the term ‘Diseases’, all results with the word ‘Diseases’ in the title, description or tagged the subject appear.
